findings.summary: A pharmacokinetic study in epileptic patients found that Shankhpushpi (Convolvulus pluricaulis) syrup co-administered with phenytoin significantly reduced phenytoin plasma trough concentrations by 50–58%, associated with breakthrough seizures in two of six patients studied; the authors attributed the reduction to CYP2C9 induction and possible drug-herb chelation in the GI tract.

Phenytoin has a narrow therapeutic index (target trough 10–20 mcg/mL); a 50% reduction in AUC places most patients below the minimum effective concentration, resulting in loss of seizure control with potentially life-threatening consequences including status epilepticus.

Multiple Ayurvedic combination formulations containing Shankhpushpi (including Mentat and Memorin) are commercially available in India and are commonly self-prescribed by epilepsy patients for cognitive support; clinicians should routinely ask about these supplements when monitoring phenytoin levels.
findings.citation: Gupta et al., Curr Drug Metab 2012
